utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 of these items. Each item is fully specified with relevant coverage details and explicit costs (monthly and annual). For example, health_coverage includes physical and mental health elements and cost breakdown; property_coverage lists multiple coverage areas and costs; travel_coverage includes detailed coverage types and costs; additional_services lists several enhancements and costs; total_budget provides a complete breakdown including monthly/annual totals. Therefore, all 5 required items are present and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three major types of insurance and one set of additional services. For health coverage, the plan chosen is 'LifeFirst Integrated Family Plan5'. The ground truth lists only 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under primary_health_coverage with ratings of 8 for both physical and mental health. Since 'LifeFirst Integrated Family Plan5' appears to be a variant of 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (same brand family and coverage scope), we can reasonably map it to the same rating level of 8. For property coverage, the plan has comprehensive features (luxury items, business equipment, art, electronics, liability, etc.) and aligns most closely with 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' under property_protection, which also has a rating of 8. For travel coverage, the plan includes extensive features such as medical evacuation, trip cancellation, business travel protection, etc. This corresponds to 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under mobility_coverage.travel_insurance, which also has a rating of 7. The additional services include telemedicine, wellness programs, international student coverage, executive stress management, and family coverage management. From the ground-truth health_enhancement_services, 'Telemedicine Services' and 'Wellness Programs' have ratings of 9 each, and 'Family Therapy Coverage' (related to family coverage management) has a rating of 8. There are no exact matches for 'international student coverage' and 'executive stress management', but they are analogous to the same domain of wellness support, so the closest mapping is still within the 8–9 range, but we only map those that exactly appear in the ground truth. Therefore, the mapped ratings are derived as follows.
